title: Apple333 infrastructure phase
objective: Plan or implement an approved isolated infrastructure change with no production-server access and no application feature expansion.

inScope: Review deployment, CI, environment, observability, or reverse-proxy configuration in the approved paths.
Create safe local validation and operator documentation.

decisions.title: Separate environment ownership
decisions.rationale: Development, staging, and production must use isolated configuration, secrets, data services, and release authority.

dependencies: Host operator confirmation for any external change

steps.description: Inspect the affected local configuration and document the operational boundary.
steps.expectedOutcome: A change plan that does not assume production access.

steps.description: Add or update safe configuration, validation, and rollback documentation only in approved paths.
steps.expectedOutcome: A reviewable infrastructure change with explicit safety controls.

steps.description: Run local syntax and application quality checks that do not contact production.

nonGoals: Do not connect to a production host.
Do not deploy, restart, or alter live services.

manualChecks: Review operator-facing safety instructions and rollback boundaries.
Confirm no environment file or live credential was accessed.

deliverables.description: Documents prerequisites, validation, rollback, and operator-owned production steps.

risks.description: Infrastructure edits can disrupt runtime availability or expose secrets when applied without operator review.
risks.mitigation: Keep validation local, require human approval, and leave all production actions to the designated operator.

acceptanceCriteria.statement: The infrastructure change is locally validated and documents clear production handoff steps.
No production access, database change, or secret exposure occurs.

stopConditions: Stop when a host, cloud account, certificate, secret, or live service action is required.
Stop when the proposed change would affect a path outside the approved infrastructure scope.
